titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 71

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=128 status=live modified=2021-04-01 18:52:48.838147+00 | translation moa=129 status=live modified=2016-06-07 15:09:36.753586+00

div.p:
Ricardus . dei gratia ecclesie sancti Andree humilis minister . uniuersis sancte matris ecclesię filiis clericis ⁊ laicis . salutem . Quoniam ad officium nostrum nouimus spectare in ęcclesia dei edi
ficare . & que edificata sunt in melius emendare . dignumduximus . ea que in ecclesia Dunfermelinensi confirmanda erant . sub pacis nostre tranquillitate suscipere
& carta nostra confirmare . Dominica igitur moniti & freti auctoritate tam absentibus quam presentibus notificamus nos concessisse ⁊ carta nostra confirmasse ut prefata
ęcclesia ęcclesias & earum possessiones ⁊ omnia ecclesiastica beneficia que eidem largitione regum pontificum ⁊ aliorum fidelium in elemosinam largita fuerint  ita
libere ⁊ honorifice ⁊ quiete ab omni seruitio & exactione possideat . sicuti aliqua ęcclesia melius ⁊ honorificentius ⁊ liberius ⁊ quietius possessiones suas in toto episcopatu
nostro tenet & possidet . uidelicet ęcclesiam de pert . ⁊ scolam eiusdem uillę . ⁊ capellam de castello . ⁊ ecclesiam sancti leonardi . ⁊ ecclesiam de uilla Glinen . ⁊ ecclesiam
de Striuelin . ⁊ scolam eiusdem uillę . ⁊ capellam de castello ⁊ cetera que iuste ad eas pertinent . ⁊ ecclesiam de kellin . ⁊ ecclesiam de Abercrumbi . ⁊ ecclesiam de Nithbren
⁊ ecclesiam de kilglessin . ⁊ capellam de inuerchethin . ⁊ ecclesiam de inueresck . ⁊ capellam de covslande . ⁊ decimas de keth . ⁊ ecclesiam de Wimet . ⁊ ecclesiam
de hala cum omnibus apendiciis suis . ⁊ ecclesiam de Neutune . ⁊ ecclesiam de Wehtercaledoure . saluo iure episcopali . ⁊ consuetudine . Testibus his . G . episcopo de
Dunkelde . W . abbate . de sancta cruce . W . priore de sancto Andrea . M . archidiacono . A . archidiacono . laud’ . Aiulfo decano . Iohanne nepote Roberti . episcopi . Roberto . filio Sevlfi
magistro hereberto . A . capellano . magistro Bartholomeo . magistro Turpin . magistro Abraam . Symone capellano . archidiaconi .

p.cl: Richard, by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, clerk and lay, Greeting:
Since we know that it is our responsibility to build up the Church of God, and to change for the better those things that have been built,

we have judged worthy to be confirmed those things that belong to the church of Dunfermline, to take them under the tranquillity of our peace and to confirm them by our charter. Instructed and supported by the Lord’s authority, we have notified those absent as well as those present that we have granted, and by our charter made firm, that the aforementioned church should possess the churches and their possessions, and all ecclesiastical benefices that have been bestowed in alms to the same by the generosity of kings, prelates, and other faithful people, as freely and honourably and quit from every service and exaction as any church well and honourably and freely and quietly holds and possesses its possessions in the whole of our bishopric, namely, the church of Perth, and the school of the same toun, the castle chapel, and the church of St Leonard, the church of Friarton, the church of Stirling, the school of the same toun, and castle chapel, and the rest of the things rightfully belonging to them, the church of Kellie, and the church of Crombie, and the church of Newburn, and the church of Kinglassie, and the chapel of Inverkeithing, and the church of Inveresk, and the chapel of Cousland, and the teinds of Keith, the church of Woolmet, and the church of Hailes with all its belongings, and the church of Newton, and the church of West Calder, saving episcopal right and custom.

With these witnesses: Gregory, bishop of Dunkeld; William, abbot of Holyrood; Walter, prior of St Andrews; Matthew, archdeacon; Andrew, archdeacon of Lothian; Aiulfo, dean; John, nephew of Bishop Robert; Roberto, son of Seolf; Master Herbert; Alexander, chaplain; Master Bartholomew; Master Turpin; Master Abraham; Simon, the archdeacon’s chaplain.
